_meta.description: Third-party-grounded benchmark pack harvested from the Google Fact Check Tools API (ClaimReview markup from AP/Reuters/Snopes/PolitiFact/AFP/BBC/...). The repo's FIRST non-self-authored pack. Domain: contemporary claims (vaccines/climate/politics/science+history misconceptions) — NOT historical authorship, so this is a NEW capability axis, not validation of the dolphin provenance delta. Labels normalized from free-form textualRating; only 'false'/'true' feed eval scoring (clean ground truth); 'mixed'/'other' retained for audit, excluded from scoring.

claims.claim: “There’s a study that came out last week of 47,000 9-year-olds in the Medicaid system in Florida — I think a Louisiana scientist called Mawson — that shows the opposite," meaning that vaccines cause autism.
GLP-1 drugs are "the first front-line intervention" to treat obesity "for 6-year-old kids"
"The Cochrane Collaboration has said that the "gold standard" approach for treating opioid addiction is 12 step programs."
The National Institutes of Health spent “almost nothing” on researching the causes of chronic disease, focusing instead on infectious disease.
“We brought that petition after CDC recommended COVID vaccine without any scientific basis for 6-year-old children."
Research “suggests that Blacks need fewer antigens" in their vaccines.

"Conditions like autism used to be one in 10,000. Now here in the state of California it is one in 22.Conditions like autism used to be one in 10,000. Now here in the state of California it is one in 22."

“Pharmaceutical medicine has its place, but no single safety study can assess the cumulative impact of one prescription on top of another prescription, and one shot on top of another shot on top of another shot, throughout the course of childhood."

"1989 is the year the [autism] epidemic began. It’s a red line. And 1989 was the year the vaccine schedule exploded. That doesn’t mean that’s a correlation. It does not mean causation, but it is something that should be looked at.”
Research shows that only a “very, very small portion” of the increase in autism “can be charged to better recognition or better diagnostic criteria.”
There is an “epidemic” of autism that must be due to an “environmental toxin.”
Researchers in 1987 in North Dakota “conducted in-person assessments of the entire population of 180,000 children under 18” in an “exhaustive” study of autism prevalence.
As part of a 1970 autism prevalence study, “all the kids in Wisconsin were tested.”
At most 25% of the increase in autism prevalence “can be attributed to better recognition and better diagnosis,” which means “85% are still — or 75% to 80% — still are part of an epidemic.”
A study testing COVID-19 vaccines in pregnant rats supports the idea that "vaccines and autism have always been linked." False.
"The Amish are a perfect example of a large group of people who are largely unvaccinated and there's no autism."
"The claim 'vaccines do not cause autism' is not an evidence-based claim"
claims.claimant: US Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

claims.claim: FDA ANNOUNCED THAT VACCINES ARE CAUSING AUTISM! Is this true?
"There is some concern that the rise in autism is the result of the preservatives that are in our vaccines."
Covid-19 vaccines cause autism
“Another Conspiracy Proven True!” referring to 2018 remarks by Pfizer CEO and claims about microchips in COVID-19 vaccines.
COVID-19 vaccines have a microchip that "tracks the location of the patient."
Videos of magnets sticking to arms prove that the COVID-19 vaccines contain microchips.
"A new report from '60 Minutes' includes an interview with a scientist from the Pentagon who says that there is now a COVID microchip."
A microchip reader for pets detected a chip in the arm of a woman vaccinated against COVID-19.
"Bill Gates will use microchip implants to fight coronavirus."
A patent held by Moderna proves its COVID mRNA vaccine contains “programmable” nanotechnology that can interact with 5G communications technology.
A video appears to show a phone detecting a new “product” when placed near a man’s arm, suggesting he was implanted with an electronic device when vaccinated.
claims.rating_raw: Vaccines do not contain microchips. This is not evidence of microchips being contained in vaccines.

claims.claim: Video of microchip found in Pfizer vaccine
Magnets can stick to your arm after a Covid-19 jab because they contain a microchip or other metals.
claims.rating_raw: This is false. The vaccines do not contain a microchip and there is nothing in a vaccine that could cause a magnet to stick to your skin after getting it.

claims.claim: Bill Gates’ “ultimate goal” is to microchip COVID-19 vaccine in order to create virtual IDs
COVID-19 vaccines contain microchips
New microchip technology could fit inside a vaccine needle
Microsoft announced it was starting human trials in June 2022 on a microchip that will store COVID-19 information.
Covid-19 vaccines contain magnetic microchips
A video shows Pfizer CEO Albert Bourla saying that the COVID-19 vaccines contain microchips.
COVID-19 vaccines contain microchips or other tracking devices, cause magnetic reaction
A viral video shows a pet scanner detecting a microchip from the COVID-19 vaccine in a person's arm.
The government is implanting microchips into human bodies through COVID-19 vaccine in order to take them under control
An implanted microchip in the body can replace vaccine cards and will be used instead to carry COVID-19 vaccine passports.
The EG.5 variant of Covid-19 is a new 5G virus.
claims.rating_raw: This is not true. Covid-19 is caused by the SARS-CoV-2 virus. The 5G network uses radio waves that do not harm people’s health.

claims.claim: It’s ironic the name of the new Covid subvariant is ‘Kraken’ a ‘mythical beast that kills on the waves’ as it’s really 5G waves that are harming and killing people.
claims.rating_raw: There is no evidence that 5G technology is harmful to human health. The name ‘Kraken’ was chosen by a Canadian biology professor in an effort to make it easier to distinguish between different subvariants.

claims.claim: Coronavirus is a result of 5G
A 5G tower powered by a “Delta” power system, suggests a link between 5G and Covid-19.
claims.rating_raw: The power system is supplied by Delta Group, an infrastructure business founded in 1971. The name is coincidental, and has no link to Covid-19.

claims.claim: Covid-19 vaccines contain Marburg virus which will be activated by 5G.
claims.rating_raw: There is no evidence for Marburg virus being in the Covid vaccines. 5G does not cause viral illnesses.

claims.claim: 5G WiFi networks could be responsible for the rapid spread of the new coronavirus.
claims.rating_raw: This is not true. There is no evidence that 5G WiFi networks are linked to the new coronavirus.

claims.claim: Covid-19 is bacterial, not viral
China was the first place to have over 100,000 5G towers.
claims.rating_raw: While it is true that China has over 100,000 towers, it is unclear if it was the first place to pass this number.

claims.claim: Bill Gates created the new coronavirus and a vaccine for it.
claims.rating_raw: This is incorrect. The Bill Gates and Melinda Gates Foundation has funded a research institute that has a patent to a different coronavirus.  Coronavirus is a large category of viruses that includes the common cold, SARS and the new virus that causes Covid-19.

claims.claim: Symptoms of Covid-19 include nausea, hair loss, bone marrow damage.
claims.rating_raw: This is incorrect. Symptoms have been reported as including fever, coughing and shortness of breath.

claims.claim: 5G is harmful to humans.
claims.rating_raw: There is no evidence that 5G can harm people.

claims.claim: The new coronavirus is actually the impact of 5G exposure.
claims.rating_raw: This is incorrect, the new coronavirus is a virus and there is no evidence that 5G is harmful to people’s health.

claims.claim: The Wuhan coronavirus is a more virulent version of the normal cold.
claims.rating_raw: The new coronavirus observed in Wuhan is a virus in the same family of viruses as the common cold, but is a different illness.

claims.claim: 5G wrecks immune systems and that is why people in Wuhan are suffering with this illness.
claims.rating_raw: There is no evidence that 5G can harm humans, or their immune systems.

claims.claim: Wuhan is where 5G was rolled out first.
claims.rating_raw: Wuhan was one of the first places with 5G trials, as well as several other large Chinese cities, like Beijing, Shanghai and Guangzhou but we don’t know if it was the first.

claims.claim: Maps show a correlation between confirmed COVID-19 cases and where 5G wireless service has been installed.
The second wave of COVID-19 was caused due to testing of 5G testing
5G radiation is the cause behind the second wave of coronavirus pandemic in India
"Another study has confirmed hydroxychloroquine to be effective in the treatment of COVID-19."
“Hydroxychloroquine — I don’t know, it’s looking like it’s having some good results. That would be a phenomenal thing.”
"You don't need masks. There is a cure. I know they don't want to open schools. No, you don't need people to be locked down. There is prevention and there is a cure."
claims.rating_raw: This is false. There is no cure for coronavirus. Regarding the use of hydroxychloroquine, the World Health Organization says: "While several drug trials are ongoing, there is currently no proof that hydroxychloroquine or any other drug can cure or prevent COVID-19."

claims.claim: A viral video claims of COVID-19, "Nobody needs to get sick. This virus has a cure. It is called hydroxychloroquine, zinc and Zithromax."

claims.claim: "The Bolivian Ministry of Health approved the use of chlorine dioxide"
claims.rating_raw: The post is fake and the Bolivian government denied the claim

claims.claim: "In the wake of the recent mass gatherings Americans have witnessed in the streets of Portland and Seattle, we are also tracking a significant rise in cases in both metropolitan areas because of what’s been going on."

claims.rating_raw: The video contains a wide range of false information, much of which has been knocked down multiple times by doctors and researchers who study the coronavirus

claims.claim: “So the so-called HHS Whistleblower was against HYDROXYCHLOROQUINE. Then why did he make, and sign, an emergency use authorization? ... A dumb @60Minutes hit job on a grandstanding Never Trumper!”

Hydroxychloroquine cures Covid-19
Vitamin D, Ivermectin, zinc and hydroxychloroquine are safe and effective treatments for Covid-19.
claims.rating_raw: There is no robust evidence yet that any of these can treat or prevent Covid-19.

claims.claim: Dr. Anthony Fauci is associated with the Virology Journal, which in 2005 published a paper suggesting chloroquine and hydroxychloroquine are a “cure and vaccine” for diseases caused by coronaviruses
Hydroxychloroquine and chloroquine are cures for the new coronavirus.

claims.claim: Hydroxychloroquine is used to treat radiation sickness.

claims.claim: “As the news tries to fear-monger about the measles ‘outbreak’ of 9 whole people, remember that the vaccine is more dangerous than the actual illness.”

claims.claim: A vaccine monitoring site shows Covid vaccines cause miscarriages.

claims.claim: “Head of Pfizer research: Covid vaccine is female sterilization.”

claims.claim: “Japan drops vax rollout, goes to Ivermectin.”

“Aren’t [vaccines] supposed to protect the vulnerable people from disease? [Vaccines] can actually spread disease. Maybe’ you’ve heard of it, it’s known as shedding."
COVID-19 vaccine “shedding” causes reproductive issues in unvaccinated people.
The COVID-19 vaccine is “shedding” from person to person. As a result, unvaccinated people who are in close proximity to vaccinated people are having changes in their period or miscarriages.

You can get side effects of Covid-19 vaccines simply by being in close proximity to someone who has had the vaccine.
claims.rating_raw: This is false. There is nothing in the Covid-19 vaccines used in the UK that could cause vaccine shedding.

claims.claim: White pine tea contains suramin and shikimic acid, which can prevent COVID-19 vaccinated people from “shedding” the spike protein
The COVID-19 vaccines and side effects from them can “shed” to affect unvaccinated people.
The US FDA has admitted unvaccinated people are at risk from people "shedding" COVID-19 mRNA vaccines.
claims.rating_raw: False. The FDA has not admitted this, and experts say there's no mechanism for this to occur.

claims.claim: Messenger RNA is transferring from the vaccinated to the unvaccinated
Individuals vaccinated against COVID-19 can shed viral components that are capable of infecting and harming those around them.

COVID-19 vaccine shedding real, meaning it can spread from person to person.
People fully vaccinated against COVID-19 can ‘shed’ viral particles from the vaccine and infect unvaccinated people
Polio vaccine linked to hand foot and mouth disease
COVID Vaccine Spike Proteins Shedding From Vaccinated To Unvaccinated Children
Drinking pine needle tea can protect people from contracting harmful particles/spike proteins that are shed from vaccinated people.
The COVID-19 death toll is exaggerated because “the state” has instructed that “anyone who didnt die by a gun shot wound or car accident” be listed as a coronavirus victim.
Because of a change in death certificate procedures specific to COVID-19, "there is a huge chance that COVID death numbers are exaggerated, to the tune of 94%."
More than twice as many people died of Covid-19 in July and August 2022 than in the same months in 2021 and 2020.
claims.rating_raw: This is misleading, because it uses a measure of Covid deaths that includes many people who died for other reasons in 2022. Overall, far fewer people have died of Covid in 2022 compared with 2021 or 2020.

claims.claim: "Many people every year, sometimes over 100,000, and despite the Vaccine, die from the Flu."
No Americans have died from the flu in 2020
“You know, years ago they used to call it global cooling."
"Coal is okay, they actually have methods now where coal becomes clean coal.”
"The wind doesn’t work ... It’s the most expensive energy."
"Every nine years you have to replace the [wind] turbines. You know, they’re made out of steel and they wear out.”
"They don’t call it [global warming] anymore, they call it climate change because, you know, some parts of the planet are cooling and warming."
Scientists "have no idea what’s going to happen [with climate change]. It’s weather."
Scientists predicted "we have 12 years to live" because of climate change.
Judith Curry gets no media coverage and she says climate changes is a "hoax."
“The climate change agenda is a hoax … The reality is more people are dying of bad climate change policies than they are of actual climate change.”
"I'm not denying climate change. But it could very well go back."
Scientists have had to pause the Climate Change Hoax Scam
claims.rating_raw: Misleading: One post even featured a GIF that the user believed showed new land emerging off the coast of Dubai due to falling sea levels — apparently unaware of the artificial Palm Islands constructed there between 2001 and 2007. The findings in the Chinese study are based on publicly available data from NASA's Gravity Recovery and Climate Experiment satellites, which have been measuring the Earth's gravitational field since 2002 and have documented changes in the planet's ice and water masses. The data may be correct, but its interpretation by conspiratorial social media users is not — a situation not helped by the researchers' decision to insert an increasing average trend curve next to the preceding decreasing curve depicting ice mass.  "This is perfect fodder for people who are intentionally looking to spread disinformation," said Johannes Feldmann, a physicist at the Potsdam Institute for Climate Impact Research near Berlin.  Feldmann emphasized that climate science relies on long-term data — typically over 20 to 30 years — to identify meaningful trends. "Two, three or even five years are far too little to identify a long-term trend," he explained.  Cherry-picking short-term data is a common tactic among climate change deniers. "There are always phases where the increase [in temperature] levels off a bit, which people suddenly take to mean: global warming has stopped, the trend is reversing," Feldmann added. "But it's never turned out to be true."

claims.claim: "The amount of carbon dioxide in the atmosphere is actually close to an all-time low ever in human history."
An ocean temperature decrease between 2013-2022 proves global warming is a hoax.
claims.rating_raw: Misleading. The claim is based on cherry-picked data and evidence shows global warming is real.

claims.claim: “Climate change is not happening."
Says "Donald Trump says climate change is a hoax invented by the Chinese."
Man-made climate change is a hoax
Earth is in a cooling phase, which questions scientific proof behind human-driven global warming
Climate change is a political hoax
“NASA admits that man-made climate change is a hoax!”
A study into global warming proves it is a hoax.
claims.rating_raw: False. The study detected no change in the rate of temperatures rises, but it found the globe has indeed been warming for decades.

claims.claim: CO2 has negligible effects on warming
Says human activity, or carbon dioxide emissions, is not the “primary contributor” to global warming.
Carbon dioxide is good for the growth of crops, it cannot be harmful to the environment or cause climate change
Carbon dioxide has an atmospheric residence time of less than a decade.
claims.rating_raw: False. Carbon dioxide stays in the atmosphere for up to thousands of years.

claims.claim: Humans are responsible for just three per cent of the carbon dioxide in the earth’s atmosphere.
claims.rating_raw: False. Humans are responsible for a third of the carbon dioxide in the earth’s atmosphere.

claims.claim: ‘No correlation’ between carbon dioxide emissions and the climate
Human-generated CO2 is an insignificant contributor to climate change
Most carbon emissions have nothing to do with fossil fuels.
Viral posts have been making claims that man-made CO2 constitutes only 0.1 to 0.2% of atmospheric greenhouse gases.
Human CO2 emissions too small to be cause of global warming
Man-made CO2 is an insignificant contributor to climate change
Experts point out that CO2 emissions increase the temperature of the planet and cause the greenhouse effect
claims.claimant: La caca del mono sonlos efectos secundarios de las vacus

claims.claim: CO2 from humans is 3.2% of total CO2, 0.12% of all greenhouse gases
Past temperatures prove CO2 cannot drive global warming
Greenhouse CO2 supplementation shows climate change is a 'scam'
Rise in atmospheric CO2 is tiny
The amount of carbon dioxide in the atmosphere is the same today as it was 150 years ago.
claims.rating_raw: False. Atmospheric carbon dioxide has increased from 0.029 per cent in 1873 to 0.042 per cent in 2023.

claims.claim: Carbon dioxide is not "a primary contributor to the global warming that we see."
Scientists are exaggerating dangers of carbon dioxide increase
Human-based carbon dioxide emissions do not cause global warming.
claims.rating_raw: False. Human activity is causing climate change due to an increase in greenhouse gases.

claims.claim: Global warming is natural, greenhouse effect small, no CO2 climate effect
Video shows "illegal cutout windows" in Maricopa County ballot envelopes to easily identify Republican ballots for election fraud purposes.
Photo shows election fraud in the ongoing West Bengal Assembly elections.

President Trump said the U.S. is "now the only Country in the World that uses Mail-in Voting.”
The Department of Government Efficiency found evidence that thousands of noncitizens illegally registered to vote and many "who did vote."
"people that voted by mail are doing the Track My Vote thing, all 20 million that voted D that have reported so far say they were told they didn't vote ... 20 million missing Democrat votes."

Donald Trump said in a video that mpox is going to be used to help commit election fraud.

Mail-in ballots show 'how many fraudulent ballots they need to produce'

claims.rating_raw: This figure is not reliable, based on the details reported. The Conservatives’ analysis reportedly compares 2025 prison data to 2021 population statistics which are likely out of date, and appears to use data on the imprisonment of foreign nationals in general to draw conclusions about small boat arrivals specifically.

claims.claim: Ukrainians Have Become Leaders in Migrant Crime in Germany
claims.rating_raw: This claim is not true. The largest group of migrants suspected of crimes are individuals from Syria (19.2% of all migrant suspects). Ukrainians accounted for only 11.6% of suspects in 2023. This figure is significantly lower than their share of all refugees living in Germany (33.1%).

claims.claim: Rising crime rates in Canada due to immigration
Asylum seekers in the UK make up 14.3% of all suspects in “crimes against life”, 12.2% in sexual offences, 11.4% in thefts and 9.7% in body injuries.
claims.rating_raw: There is no evidence for this, though official data on this topic is limited. These figures are actually from Germany in 2017 and include asylum seekers and some specific immigration categories. The Office for National Statistics and Home Office do not publish equivalent UK data, so we don’t know what the correct figures are.
